page: Amazon Web Services c8gn.8xlarge is a compute-optimized server powered by custom AWS Graviton processors built on the 64-bit ARMv9 architecture. It features 32 dedicated physical cores and 32 vCPUs running at 2.8 GHz with a Neoverse-V2 core design. The processor includes 64 KB of L1 data cache, 2 MB of L2 cache, and 36 MB of L3 cache. The instance is equipped with 64.0 GB of system memory, providing a resource density of 2.0 GB per core. It operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor and does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware.

This server type is optimized for networking and EBS performance, delivering a baseline network bandwidth of 100 Gbps. Benchmark data indicates top-tier memory performance, with uncached large-block read and write memory bandwidth scoring in the top 10% of tested systems. Geekbench multi-core performance and PassMark database operations also rank in the top 10%, while Geekbench single-core, text processing, and image processing benchmarks fall into the top 25%. However, large-scale LLM inference is a performance tradeoff, with prompt processing for a 70B model ranking in the bottom 25%.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the ARM64 architecture and dedicated core allocation offer high resource density and memory throughput without the overhead of simultaneous multithreading. The lack of bundled local storage means storage requirements must be met via network-attached volumes. This instance is best suited for network-intensive and compute-bound workloads, including high-throughput database operations, static web serving, software compilation, and multi-threaded data decompression.
description: Amazon Web Services c8gn.8xlarge is an ARM64 compute-optimized server powered by a 32-core AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processor running at 2.8 GHz. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, it features 32 dedicated physical cores, 64.0 GB of system memory, and a baseline network bandwidth of 100 Gbps, with no local storage or GPU hardware. Benchmark results highlight top-tier memory bandwidth and database operations, alongside strong multi-core performance in Geekbench and software compilation. However, large-model LLM inference presents a performance tradeoff, scoring in the bottom 25% for 70B model prompt processing. This instance provides qualitative cost efficiency for high-throughput, network-bound, and compute-intensive workloads. It is highly suited for database operations, static web serving, software compilation, and multi-threaded decompression tasks.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c8gn.8xlarge is an ARM64 compute-optimized server featuring 32 dedicated Graviton cores, 64 GB of RAM, 100 Gbps network bandwidth, and top-tier memory performance.

tagline: A dedicated 32-core ARM64 compute-optimized instance delivering 100 Gbps network bandwidth and top-tier memory throughput for demanding workloads.

64.0 GB of system memory providing 2.0 GB per core
100 Gbps baseline network bandwidth optimized for high throughput
